---
title: Agent-assisted release workflow
---

This guide shows where coding agents can help when someone creates a project from this template and prepares a first release.

## Deployment flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Use the template] --> B[Project setup<br/>Codespaces or local]
    B --> C[Agent-assisted repository updates<br/>placeholders, docs, metadata, changelog]
    C --> D[Validation<br/>format, lint, preview, link checks]
    D --> E[GitHub Pages publication]
    E --> F[GitHub release asset<br/>site-tag.zip]
    F --> G[Zenodo archive and DOI]
```

### Where agents can intervene

- **Use the template**: agents can explain the repository structure and identify which files still contain placeholders.
- **Project setup**: agents can edit metadata, documentation, and support files after following [.github/copilot-instructions.md](../.github/copilot-instructions.md).
- **Validation**: agents can run formatting and changelog commands, then prepare preview-ready documentation changes for review.
- **Publication**: maintainers should approve GitHub Pages, release publication, and Zenodo steps, while agents prepare the repository state and release notes.

## Showcase examples and automation opportunities

The showcase links below come from the
["One Template to Rule Them All"](https://maehr.github.io/one-template-to-rule-them-all/)
presentation.

| Showcase | What the template supports | Where agents help |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Stadt.Geschichte.Basel RDM](https://dokumentation.stadtgeschichtebasel.ch/) | Public-facing FAIR documentation for a research data platform | Update metadata, refresh docs, and keep checklists and support files aligned |
| [sgb-figures](https://dokumentation.stadtgeschichtebasel.ch/sgb-figures/) | Publication-ready visualisation documentation | Synchronize narrative text, figures, changelog entries, and formatting |
| [nordatlantisk-ft](http://mtwente.github.io/nordatlantisk-ft/) | Scraping and analysis workflow documentation | Scaffold scripts, document pipeline changes, and prepare release notes |
| [maxvogt-analysis](https://mtwente.github.io/maxvogt-analysis/) | Multi-step data compilation workflow | Keep metadata edits and processing docs synchronized across steps |
| [Modelling Marti](https://mtwente.github.io/modelling-marti) | Narrative project with interactive outputs | Automate notebook-to-doc updates, link checks, and preview validation |
| [Digital History Switzerland 2024](https://digihistch24.github.io/) | Event website with schedules and abstracts | Batch-edit content, validate links, and update release notes |
| [Digital Humanities Bern](https://dhbern.github.io/) | Department site with news and events | Maintain navigation, content consistency, and formatting checks |
| [Decoding Inequality 2025](https://dhbern.github.io/decoding-inequality-2025/) | Teaching site with course materials | Update materials, metadata, and changelog entries together |
| [Non-discriminatory Metadata](https://maehr.github.io/diskriminierungsfreie-metadaten/) | Living handbook and publication | Keep citations, docs, and accessibility-focused content aligned |
| [One Template to Rule Them All](https://maehr.github.io/one-template-to-rule-them-all/) | Meta-example for this repository family | Reuse the same setup, validation, and release workflow documented here |

## First-release checklist handoff

1. Use [TODO.md](../TODO.md) to split work into 🤖, 👤, and 🤝 tasks.
2. Generate a draft changelog with `npm run changelog`.
3. Review the preview before any production publication step.
4. Publish GitHub Pages and the first release outside the agent session.
5. After Zenodo records the release, update `ZENODO_RECORD`, `DOI`, and the DOI badge placeholders in the project instance.
